question: Q1
title: SQL Injection in Product Search via Template Literal
exact_code: models.sequelize.query(`SELECT * FROM Products WHERE ((name LIKE '%${criteria}%' OR description LIKE '%${criteria}%') AND deletedAt IS NULL) ORDER BY name`)
data_flow: req.query.q → criteria variable → template literal interpolation → SQL query
exploit_scenario: Attacker sets ?q='; DROP TABLE Products; -- to execute arbitrary SQL commands
fix_recommendation: Use parameterized queries with replacements array

question: Q1
title: SQL Injection in Login Authentication via Template Literal
exact_code: models.sequelize.query(`SELECT * FROM Users WHERE email = '${req.body.email || ''}' AND password = '${security.hash(req.body.password || '')}' AND deletedAt IS NULL`, { model: UserModel, plain: true })
data_flow: req.body.email → template literal interpolation → SQL query (password is hashed but email is not)
exploit_scenario: Attacker sends POST with email='admin'-- to bypass authentication
framework_protection: partial (password is hashed, but email field is vulnerable)
fix_recommendation: Use parameterized queries with replacements array

question: Q3
title: NoSQL Injection in Order Tracking via $where Operator
data_flow: req.params.id → template literal → MongoDB $where operator (JavaScript code evaluation)
exploit_scenario: Attacker sets id='; this.orderId == true; // to return all orders
framework_protection: partial (id is truncated but $where still evaluates JavaScript)
fix_recommendation: Use normal MongoDB query without $where: find({ orderId: id })

question: Q4
title: Code Evaluation in B2B Order Processing via safeEval
exploit_scenario: Attacker may bypass notevil sandbox to achieve RCE via crafted orderLinesData payload
framework_protection: partial (notevil library used but vm.runInContext is inherently risky)
fix_recommendation: Avoid eval entirely; parse orderLinesData as JSON and validate structure instead

question: Q3
title: NoSQL Injection in Product Reviews via $where Operator
data_flow: req.params.id → string concatenation → MongoDB $where operator (JavaScript code evaluation)
exploit_scenario: Attacker sets id='1 || true ||' to return all reviews
framework_protection: partial (id is truncated but $where still evaluates JavaScript)
fix_recommendation: Use normal MongoDB query without $where: find({ product: Number(id) })

question: Q5
title: Open Redirect via Allowlist Bypass (Substring Matching)
data_flow: query.to → isRedirectAllowed() → url.includes() substring check → res.redirect()
exploit_scenario: Attacker uses url='https://github.com.attacker.com/juice-shop/juice-shop' to bypass allowlist via substring match
fix_recommendation: Use URL hostname validation instead of substring matching
